method_atom: An online sequential-decision framework in which, at each round, one option is chosen from a large or possibly infinite candidate set and yields a noisy scalar payoff that is a linear function of a shared, unknown low-dimensional parameter vector; the goal is to minimize cumulative loss relative to the best option. The analysis pairs an information-theoretic lower bound on achievable loss with a matching near-optimal policy that alternates dedicated exploration and exploitation phases, giving cumulative loss that scales as the parameter dimension times the square root of the horizon length.
object_atom: A bandit problem with a large (possibly infinite) collection of arms whose expected rewards are linear in a shared r-dimensional random vector, studied for specific arm-set geometries: arms on the unit sphere, strongly convex arm sets, and general arm sets.
brief_reason: The linear-payoff sequential exploration/exploitation loss-minimization machinery with matching lower and upper bounds is field-neutral and transferable, while the arms and their geometric structure (unit sphere, convex sets) are the domain-specific object; bandit problems fuse method and object fairly closely.
